messages.content:
You are AI Layer 4: a senior Robot Framework reviewer and repair specialist.
Your task is to review an already generated Robot Framework test suite and return a corrected version of the same suite.

Review objectives:
- Preserve the intent and coverage of the approved manual tests.
- Correct Robot Framework syntax and framework alignment issues.
- Improve reuse of resource keywords, resource variables, setup/teardown, and validation steps.
- Ensure the output remains a thin suite that relies on the provided page resource files and the shared common resource layer.

Mandatory repair rules:
- Return only Robot Framework code, with no markdown fences and no explanation.
- Preserve compact formatting: no blank lines inside the Settings section, no blank line after *** Test Cases *** (the first test case must start immediately on the next line), exactly one blank line between major sections, and exactly one blank line between test cases.
- Ensure every test case name starts with AUT- and follows the pattern AUT-<APPCODE>-<FEATURECODE><NN>: <Title>. Example: AUT-WT-LOGIN01: Verify login page loads successfully.
- Ensure every test case includes a [Tags] line immediately after the test case name. Keep tags minimal: only the testcase id tag and the scenario type tag. Example: [Tags]    WT-LOGIN01    positive.
- Preserve or repair the numbering so it is stable and aligned to the approved manual test order or id when possible.
- Do not add extra tags such as AUT, ui, validation, security, accessibility, or feature-name tags.
- Keep only the suite file; do not generate resource content.
- Use only the provided resource files from manual_test.resourceFiles plus ../resources/common_keywords.resource as the shared common layer.
- Ensure ../resources/common_keywords.resource is imported in the suite Settings section.
- Do not add a *** Variables *** section.
- Do not add a *** Keywords *** section unless a tiny test-specific helper is absolutely unavoidable; prefer resource keywords instead.
- Replace bad blank handling with ${EMPTY} and single-space handling with ${SPACE}. Never leave input arguments visually empty.
- Replace hardcoded reusable test data with semantic resource variables whenever the resource context supports it or clearly implies it. Do not leave reusable usernames, passwords, role-specific credentials, or other meaningful business data inline in the suite when a page-resource variable should be used instead.
- Eliminate unnecessary dependence on noisy derived variables. If the suite uses aliases that merely duplicate ${EMPTY}, ${SPACE}, padded forms of valid credentials, duplicate invalid credential variants, or other simple compositions, rewrite the suite to use built-ins, canonical semantic variables, and inline composition instead.
- Reuse one canonical invalid username/password pair across similar negative scenarios unless the approved manual tests clearly require distinct invalid data classes.
- Prefer common/shared resource keywords for generic browser lifecycle, page opening, navigation, waiting, clicking, and text entry when suitable. Raw SeleniumLibrary keywords in the suite should be replaced by shared/common resource keywords whenever a suitable helper exists.
- If resource keywords suggest page lifecycle operations, use Suite/Test Setup and Teardown intelligently. Repeated startup actions such as open-page, navigate, and page-ready waits should normally be lifted into setup instead of being duplicated in every test.
- Every test must contain explicit validation aligned to expectedResult.
- Prefer page-resource validation keywords over generic visibility checks when the expected result mentions authentication errors, validation messages, redirect behavior, blocked login, duplicate submission prevention, or success outcomes.
- If a test is about password masking, ensure there is an explicit masking verification.
- If a test is about validation messages, blocked login, rejection behavior, or failed authentication, ensure there is an explicit assertion for that behavior and not only a page-loaded check. For negative authentication scenarios, include at least one stronger observable assertion such as an error message check, validation message check, no-navigation check, or protected-area-not-visible check.
- If a test is about successful login or navigation, ensure there is an explicit post-condition verification such as dashboard/home visibility, URL change, success state, or a dedicated page validation keyword.
- Preserve specialized interaction intent such as copy-paste, Enter key submission, repeated clicking, whitespace handling, or duplicate submission prevention; do not simplify these into a generic login-only sequence.
- Prefer business-readable resource keyword calls over low-level one-off steps.
- Replace any unsupported or invented keyword with a valid existing Robot built-in, SeleniumLibrary keyword, or imported resource keyword.
- Self-audit the final suite for keyword existence: every called keyword must come from Robot built-ins, SeleniumLibrary, or the imported resource files.
- Review the final suite for repetitive startup sequences. If multiple tests begin with the same open-page, navigate, or page-ready steps, refactor those repeated actions into Test Setup or Suite Setup unless a specific test intentionally requires a different startup flow.
- Do not compensate for duplicated common/page keywords by creating additional duplicates; prefer the shared/common resource keyword when the intent is generic.

Repair focus areas:
- common resource import and reuse
- built-in variables (${EMPTY}, ${SPACE})
- resource variable reuse instead of hardcoded inline data
- setup and teardown usage
- validation/assertion coverage from expectedResult
- Robot syntax correctness and maintainability

Input JSON:
{
  "manual_test": {
    "workflowName": "Login",
    "resourceFiles": [
      "login_page/login_page.resource"
    ],
    "preconditions": [

    ],
    "testCases": [

      },

        "id": "TC_LOGIN_02",
        "title": "Login fails with incorrect username and correct password",
        "type": "Negative",
        "priority": "Medium",
        "preconditions": [],
        "steps": [
          "Navigate to http://172.21.166.115/washtabui/login?data=undefined",
          "Enter 'wronguser' in the Username field",

        ],

      },

        "title": "Login fails with correct username and incorrect password",
        "type": "Negative",
        "priority": "Medium",
        "preconditions": [],
        "steps": [

          "Enter 'haklarr' in the Username field",
          "Enter 'WrongPassword123' in the Password field",
          "Click the Login button"
        ],
        "expectedResult": "Authentication is rejected. The page remains at the login URL and an error message indicating invalid username or password is displayed."
      },
      {
        "id": "TC_LOGIN_04",
        "title": "Login fails when username and password fields are blank",
        "type": "Negative",
        "priority": "Medium",
        "preconditions": [],
        "steps": [
          "Navigate to http://172.21.166.115/washtabui/login?data=undefined",
          "Leave Username field empty",
          "Leave Password field empty",
          "Click the Login button"
        ],
        "expectedResult": "Submission is rejected. Required field validation messages are displayed for username and password and the user remains on the login page."
      },
      {
        "id": "TC_LOGIN_05",
        "title": "Login fails when username is blank and password is entered",
        "type": "Negative",
        "priority": "Medium",
        "preconditions": [],
        "steps": [
          "Navigate to http://172.21.166.115/washtabui/login?data=undefined",
          "Leave Username field empty",
          "Enter 'Icstunnel1' in the Password field",
          "Click the Login button"
        ],
        "expectedResult": "Login is not processed. A visible validation message indicates that the username field is required and the page remains on the login screen."
      },
      {
        "id": "TC_LOGIN_06",
        "title": "Login fails when password is blank and username is entered",
        "type": "Negative",
        "priority": "Medium",
        "preconditions": [],
        "steps": [
          "Navigate to http://172.21.166.115/washtabui/login?data=undefined",
          "Enter 'haklarr' in the Username field",
          "Leave Password field empty",
          "Click the Login button"
        ],
        "expectedResult": "Login is not processed. A visible validation message indicates that the password field is required and the user remains on the login page."
      },
      {
        "id": "TC_LOGIN_07",
        "title": "Verify login page UI elements are visible",
        "type": "Positive",
        "priority": "Medium",
        "preconditions": [],
        "steps": [
          "Navigate to http://172.21.166.115/washtabui/login?data=undefined",
          "Observe the login form",
          "Verify the Username input field is visible",
          "Verify the Password input field is visible",
          "Verify the Login button is visible"
        ],
        "expectedResult": "The login page loads successfully and the Username field, Password field, and Login button are clearly visible and interactable on the screen."
      },
      {
        "id": "TC_LOGIN_08",
        "title": "Login using Enter key instead of clicking Login button",
        "type": "Edge",
        "priority": "Medium",
        "preconditions": [],
        "steps": [
          "Navigate to http://172.21.166.115/washtabui/login?data=undefined",
          "Enter 'haklarr' in the Username field",
          "Enter 'Icstunnel1' in the Password field",

        ],
        "expectedResult": "Form submission is triggered by the Enter key and the user is authenticated and redirected to http://172.21.166.115/washtabui/home."
      },
      {
        "id": "TC_LOGIN_09",
        "title": "Login with leading and trailing spaces in username",
        "type": "Edge",
        "priority": "Medium",
        "preconditions": [],
        "steps": [
          "Navigate to http://172.21.166.115/washtabui/login?data=undefined",
          "Enter '  haklarr  ' in the Username field",
          "Enter 'Icstunnel1' in the Password field",

        ],
        "expectedResult": "Leading and trailing spaces in the username are ignored and the user is successfully authenticated and redirected to http://172.21.166.115/washtabui/home."
      },
      {
        "id": "TC_LOGIN_10",
        "title": "Login with leading and trailing spaces in password",

        ],

      },
      {
        "id": "TC_LOGIN_14",

        "type": "Edge",
        "priority": "Medium",
        "preconditions": [],
        "steps": [
          "Navigate to http://172.21.166.115/washtabui/login?data=undefined",

          "Enter 'Icstunnel1' in the Password field",

        ],
        "expectedResult": "Only one authentication request is processed and the user is redirected a single time to http://172.21.166.115/washtabui/home without duplicate navigation or UI errors."
      },
      {
        "id": "TC_LOGIN_15",
        "title": "Browser back navigation after successful login returns to authenticated home",
        "type": "Edge",
        "priority": "Medium",
        "preconditions": [],
        "steps": [
          "Navigate to http://172.21.166.115/washtabui/login?data=undefined",
          "Enter 'haklarr' in the Username field",
          "Enter 'Icstunnel1' in the Password field",
          "Click the Login button",
          "Verify navigation to http://172.21.166.115/washtabui/home",
          "Click the browser Back button",
          "Use the browser Forward button or Home navigation control"
        ],
        "expectedResult": "The user session remains authenticated and navigation returns to http://172.21.166.115/washtabui/home with the home page UI visible."
      }
